No Poverty.reason: The course's general mention of 'social responsibility' is too broad to establish a direct or strong link to specific poverty eradication targets without further explicit evidence.

Zero Hunger.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to ending hunger, food security, or sustainable agriculture.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to health, well-being, or medical research.

Quality Education.reason: The course is tertiary education focused on developing advanced research capabilities for academia, directly aligning with targets for equal access to quality tertiary education (4.3) and relevant skills (4.4).
evidence: 發展必要的研究能力為學術界貢獻 (Students will develop the research capabilities necessary to contribute to academia.)
習得相關進階專業知識(Students will acquire and apply expertise in their respective fields.)

Gender Equality.reason: The general mention of 'social responsibility' is insufficient to establish a direct link to specific gender equality targets without explicit evidence in the syllabus.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to energy access, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: Developing advanced research skills for academia contributes to higher economic productivity (8.2). The focus on 'social responsibility' can broadly relate to protecting labor rights (8.8).
evidence: 發展必要的研究能力為學術界貢獻 (Students will develop the research capabilities necessary to contribute to academia.)

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's core purpose is to 'develop the research capabilities necessary to contribute to academia,' directly enhancing scientific research and encouraging innovation (Target 9.5).
evidence: 發展必要的研究能力為學術界貢獻 (Students will develop the research capabilities necessary to contribute to academia.)
在專業領域能深入研究(Students will acquire professional skills.)

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The link from general 'ethics' to specific targets for reducing inequalities is too weak without explicit mention of addressing disparities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to urban planning, housing, or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: Core Competency B explicitly involves understanding 'sustainability considerations of business situations,' directly supporting sustainable practices and reporting (Target 12.6).
evidence: 理解與辨別商業環境中...永續議題 (Students will understand and be able to identify the...sustainability considerations of business situations.)

Climate Action.reason: While 'sustainability' is mentioned, there is no explicit reference to climate change, mitigation, or adaptation in the syllabus.

Life Below Water.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No evidence in the course syllabus directly or indirectly relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's focus on 'ethics' and 'social responsibility' provides a foundation for research that can contribute to developing effective and accountable institutions (Target 16.6).
evidence: 理解與辨別商業環境中道德、社會責任 (Students will understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility...of business situations.)

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: Developing academic research skills on 'sustainability considerations' supports knowledge sharing (17.16) and can enhance policy coherence for sustainable development (17.14).
evidence: 發展必要的研究能力為學術界貢獻 (Students will develop the research capabilities necessary to contribute to academia.)
